have h : Differentiable ℝ fun (x : ℝ³) => (rotR (x.ofLp 0)).comp (rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2)) := by
    -- Prove that the composition of rotations is differentiable
    refine'
      Differentiable.comp _ (differentiable_rotR_ofLp 0) _
    -- Prove that the rotation matrix is differentiable with respect to its arguments
    refine'
      (differentiable_const.comp (differentiable_id.add (differentiable_id.add differentiable_const)))
    <;>
      (try
        norm_num) <;>
      (try
        linarith) <;>
      (try
        ring_nf) <;>
      (try
        simp_all [differentiable_const, differentiable_id]) <;>
      (try
        Differentiable.add)
  -- Since the composition is differentiable, the function is differentiable
  have h₁ : Differentiable ℝ fun (x : ℝ³) => ( (rotR (x.ofLp 0)).comp (rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2)) ) S := by
    apply Differentiable.comp h (differentiable_const)
  convert h₁ using 1
  <;> simp [Function.comp_apply]
  <;> ring_nf
  <;> simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
